lines.english: The self-willed manmukh is lured by another man's wife.
The noose is around his neck, and he is entangled in petty conflicts.
The Gurmukh is emancipated, singing the Glorious Praises of the Lord. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਪਿਰ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਿ ਨ ਕਬਹੂੰ ਹੋਈ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: bin pir tripat na kabahoon hoee |6|
lines.english: , but without her husband, she is never satisfied. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਮਿ ਦਾਮਿ ਚਿਤੁ ਪਰ ਵਸਿ ਸੇਈ ॥
lines.translit: kaam daam chit par vas seee |
lines.english: she allows her mind to be controlled by others for lust or money

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਤਨੁ ਬਿਧਵਾ ਪਰ ਕਉ ਦੇਈ ॥
lines.translit: jiau tan bidhavaa par kau deee |
lines.english: The lonely widow gives her body to a stranger;
The Simritees, Vedas and Puraanas;
You may read, recite and study the scriptures,

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਰਸ ਰਾਤੇ ਮਨੁ ਬਹੁ ਨਾਟਾ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: bin ras raate man bahu naattaa |7|
lines.english: but without being imbued with the Lord's essence, the mind wanders endlessly. ||7||
As the rainbird thirsts longingly for the drop of rain,
Nanak is satisfied by the sublime essence of the Lord. ||8||11||
and as the fish delights in the water,

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਠੁ ਕਰਿ ਮਰੈ ਨ ਲੇਖੈ ਪਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: hatth kar marai na lekhai paavai |
lines.english: One who dies in stubbornness shall not be approved,
Gauree, First Mehl:
Forgetting the Naam, the Name of the Lord, he comes to regret and repent in the end. ||1||
even though he may wear religious robes and smear his body all over with ashes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੁ ਬਿਸਾਰਿ ਸਹਹਿ ਜਮ ਦੂਖ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: naam bisaar saheh jam dookh |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Forgetting the Naam, you shall have to endure the pain of death. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂੰ ਮਨਿ ਹਰਿ ਜੀਉ ਤੂੰ ਮਨਿ ਸੂਖ ॥
lines.translit: toon man har jeeo toon man sookh |
lines.english: Believe in the Dear Lord, and you shall find peace of mind.
Forgetting the Naam, one becomes the most false of all the false. ||2||
The smell of musk, sandalwood and camphor,
Lances and swords, marching bands, thrones and the salutes of others
and the intoxication of Maya, takes one far away from the state of supreme dignity.
Union with God is not obtained by arguments and egotism.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਜਾਚੇ ਭਗਤਿ ਨ ਨਾਮੁ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: bin har jaache bhagat na naam |3|
lines.english: Without seeking the Lord, neither devotional worship nor the Naam are obtained. ||3||
only increase his desire; he is engrossed in sexual desire.
In the love of duality and ignorance, you shall suffer. ||4||
But by offering your mind, the comfort of the Naam is obtained.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਦਮ ਕੇ ਸਉਦਾ ਨਹੀ ਹਾਟ ॥
lines.translit: bin dam ke saudaa nahee haatt |
lines.english: Without money, you cannot buy anything in the store.
Without a boat, you cannot cross over the ocean.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਕਉ ਵਾਹੁ ਵਾਹੁ ਜਿ ਵਾਟ ਦਿਖਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: tis kau vaahu vaahu ji vaatt dikhaavai |
lines.english: Waaho! Waaho! - Hail, hail, to the one who shows us the Way.
Without serving the Guru, everything is lost. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਕਉ ਵਾਹੁ ਵਾਹੁ ਜਿ ਮੇਲਿ ਮਿਲਾਵੈ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: tis kau vaahu vaahu ji mel milaavai |6|
lines.english: Waaho! Waaho! - Hail, hail, to the one who unites me in the Lord's Union. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਾਹੁ ਵਾਹੁ ਤਿਸ ਕਉ ਜਿਸ ਕਾ ਇਹੁ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: vaahu vaahu tis kau jis kaa ihu jeeo |
lines.english: Waaho! Waaho! - Hail, hail, to the one who is the Keeper of this soul.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਕਉ ਵਾਹੁ ਵਾਹੁ ਜਿ ਸਬਦੁ ਸੁਣਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: tis kau vaahu vaahu ji sabad sunaavai |
lines.english: Waaho! Waaho! - Hail, hail, to the one who teaches the Word of the Shabad.
Through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, contemplate this Ambrosial Nectar.
The Glorious Greatness of the Naam is bestowed according to the Pleasure of Your Will. ||7||
O Nanak, attuned to the Naam, honor is attained. ||8||12||
Without the Naam, how can I live, O mother?
Night and day, I chant it; I remain in the Protection of Your Sanctuary.
Acting in egotism, the Lord is not known, even by wearing religious robes.
Gauree, First Mehl:
How rare is that Gurmukh, who surrenders his mind in devotional worship. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਹਉ ਕਰਤ ਨਹੀ ਸਚੁ ਪਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: hau hau karat nahee sach paaeeai |
lines.english: By actions done in egotism, selfishness and conceit, the True Lord is not obtained.
The kings act in egotism, and undertake all sorts of expeditions.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉਮੈ ਜਾਇ ਪਰਮ ਪਦੁ ਪਾਈਐ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: haumai jaae param pad paaeeai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: But when egotism departs, then the state of supreme dignity is obtained. ||1||Pause||
One who restrains his fickle mind subdues the five passions. ||3||
But through their egotism, they are ruined; they die, only to be reborn over and over again. ||2||
Egotism is overcome only by contemplating the Word of the Guru's Shabad.
With the True Lord deep within the self, the Celestial Mansion is intuitively found.
Understanding the Sovereign Lord, the state of supreme dignity is obtained. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਹਉ ਕਰਿ ਮਰਣਾ ਕਿਆ ਪਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: hau hau kar maranaa kiaa paavai |
lines.english: Those who act in egotism, selfishness and conceit die; what do they gain?
They focus their attention on the Home of the Fearless Lord. ||5||
The Guru dispels the doubts of those whose actions are true.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰਾ ਗੁਰੁ ਭੇਟੇ ਸੋ ਝਗਰੁ ਚੁਕਾਵੈ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: pooraa gur bhette so jhagar chukaavai |6|
lines.english: Those who meet the Perfect Guru are rid of all conflicts. ||6||
Whatever exists, is in reality nothing.
Obtaining spiritual wisdom from the Guru, I sing the Glories of God. ||7||
